assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and an existing Git-provider account that can own or administer target repositories.
No CodeRabbit account, installed Git App, authorized repositories, or reviewed pull request exists yet.

developer_goal: Create a CodeRabbit account, authorize repositories on a supported Git platform, and receive the first actionable pull-request review.

first_success.outcome: CodeRabbit posts an actionable review on a pull request in an authorized repository.
first_success.observable_signal: After setup reaches You're all set and a pull request is opened, the pull request shows a CodeRabbit review with actionable findings.

route_selection.surface: CodeRabbit hosted Quickstart with Git-platform-specific authorization
route_selection.rule: Prefer the hosted pull-request Quickstart over CLI or IDE reviews, then resolve one Git platform only when its complete permission and signup fields are established.

candidates.name: GitHub.com hosted integration
candidates.reason: First-listed platform docs cover Login with GitHub, OAuth authorize, org selection, and Install & Authorize, but a conditional Complete your signup dialog may still collect undocumented fields.

candidates.name: GitLab.com, Azure DevOps, Bitbucket Cloud, or self-hosted Git platforms
candidates.reason: Official Quickstart and platforms overview present these as peer integrations with different ownership, service-account, and permission mechanics.

candidates.name: IDE or CLI reviews without a hosted pull request
candidates.reason: Quickstart lists IDE and CLI reviews after You're all set as alternate next actions; the documented first hosted success is pull-request review feedback.

required_path.action: Open the CodeRabbit login surface and choose a Git-platform sign-in method.

required_fields.label: Git platform sign-in method
required_fields.notes: Public login shows GitHub Cloud, GitLab Cloud, Bitbucket Cloud, Azure DevOps Cloud, and SSO.

required_fields.label: Terms of Use and Privacy Policy acknowledgement
required_fields.notes: Login states that continuing agrees to CodeRabbit Terms of Use and Privacy Policy.

required_path.observable_result: The browser redirects to the selected Git provider for authorization.

required_path.action: Authorize CodeRabbit on the selected Git platform and grant repository access.

required_fields.label: Git provider OAuth or App authorization
Repository access scope
required_fields.notes: GitHub.com documents All repositories versus Only select repositories.

required_fields.label: Complete your signup fields
required_fields.notes: Hands-on guide says a Complete your signup dialog may appear and must be filled with requested information, without listing the fields.

required_path.observable_result: CodeRabbit finishes integration setup and the product presents the post-setup You're all set state.

required_path.action: Open a pull request in an authorized repository.

required_fields.label: Pull request contents and target branch
required_fields.notes: Quickstart says open a pull request; exact create-PR fields depend on the Git platform and whether a test repository must be created first.

required_path.observable_result: A reviewable pull request exists in a repository CodeRabbit can access.

required_path.action: Open the CodeRabbit review on the pull request and confirm actionable findings.
required_path.observable_result: The pull request shows CodeRabbit review feedback with actionable findings or suggested improvements.

prerequisites.description: An existing supported Git-provider account with owner-level permissions on at least one repository; organization installs need organization owner permissions.

external_gates.description: The Git provider must grant CodeRabbit the OAuth or Git App permissions required to read code and review pull requests.

unavoidable_waits.description: After a pull request is opened, CodeRabbit needs a few minutes to analyze the changes and post the first review.

platform_outcomes.description: CodeRabbit creates the hosted account from the Git-provider sign-in and completes repository integration until You're all set.
CodeRabbit posts an automated review with actionable findings on the open pull request.

excluded.item: Reading CodeRabbit documentation and the hands-on guide narrative
excluded.reason: Documentation is evidence, not a product action.
Presented as alternate next actions after hosted setup, not the Quickstart pull-request first success.
Optional customization after default reviews already work.

sources.title: CodeRabbit Quickstart
evidence_supported: Sign up with existing Git-provider account

sources.title: CodeRabbit login
evidence_supported: Terms and privacy acknowledgement on continue

uncertainties.question: Which Git platform's authorization path is the corpus comparison route, and what fields appear if Complete your signup is shown?
uncertainties.impact: Platform choice changes permission UI, service-account requirements, and field counts; the conditional signup dialog can add undocumented required fields.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Pick one platform, then capture a dated fresh-account walkthrough from CodeRabbit login through You're all set and the first pull-request review, including any signup dialog fields.
